_meta.description: LLM gateway test fixtures — standard prompts for functional validation
_meta.note: These prompts are designed to produce deterministic or near-deterministic responses. Tests validate response FORMAT, not content — LLM output is non-deterministic by nature. The echo and format-check prompts are the most reliable for automated pass/fail.

chat_prompts.name: echo_check
chat_prompts.description: Asks model to return a specific token. Most reliable for automated pass/fail.

messages.content: Reply with exactly the word: GATEWAY_OK

chat_prompts.name: json_format_check
chat_prompts.description: Verifies model can return valid JSON — validates structured output capability.

messages.content: Return a JSON object with exactly two keys: 'status' set to 'ok' and 'service' set to 'llm-gateway'. Return only the JSON, no other text.

chat_prompts.name: japanese_language_check
chat_prompts.description: Verifies Japanese language output — relevant for Shogun use case.

messages.content: Translate 'Good morning' to Japanese. Return only the Japanese text, no romanization.

validation.note: Manual verification — automated check confirms non-empty response

chat_prompts.name: multi_turn_check
chat_prompts.description: Verifies multi-turn conversation context is maintained.

messages.content: What number did I ask you to remember? Reply with just the number.

chat_prompts.name: provider_routing_check
chat_prompts.description: Tests explicit provider routing — used to verify each provider path works.

embedding_prompts.name: basic_embedding_check
embedding_prompts.description: Verifies embedding endpoint returns a vector of expected dimensions.
embedding_prompts.note: text-embedding-3-small returns 1536 dimensions. If model changes, update this fixture.

embedding_prompts.name: batch_embedding_check
embedding_prompts.description: Verifies batch embedding — multiple inputs in one call.
